description:
The updateMulti method indicates "By default the method updates a single document." 

http://api.mongodb.org/java/current/com/mongodb/DBCollection.html#updateMulti(com.mongodb.DBObject, com.mongodb.DBObject)

Shouldn't the updateMulti update mutliple documents?

summary: Remove ReadPreference methods that use DBObject to describe replica set tags lists
Remove CommandFailureException construct and getCommandResult method

description: This class was intended for internal use only and does not belong in the public API.

summary: Deprecate tag-related methods in ReadPreference that use DBObject
description: Introduce use of TagSet class as an alternative.

This needs to be deprecated because whether continue on error is true is orthogonal to the write concern.  This can be seen in the bulk write API, where whether the writes are ordered is specified separately from the write concern.

summary: Provide a way to specify continueOnError for multi-document insert without having to use the property on write concern
description: In preparation for deprecating and subsequently removing WriteConcern.continueOnError property, adding DBCollection.insert methods that take a boolean or enumerated value indicating whether it should continue on error.
This property was deprecated in 2.13, and will be removed in 3.0

summary: Replace Tags with TagSet/Tag in ReadPreference class
description: Replace Tags with Tag and TagSet to be in sync with upcoming changes in 2.13.0.
CreateIndexesOperation should test creating a unique index where a DuplicateKey exception would stop the index being created.
This method should be seldom used, as the only option without a type-safe getter/setter is jsMode.  After one is added for jsMode in JAVA-1347, these methods should be deprecated.
Remove MapReduceCommand.addExtraOption and getExtraOptions in 3.0, as typesafe getter/setter methods are preferred and safer, given that MapReduceOperation is entirely typed.

summary: Add type-safe jsMode support in MapReduceCommand
description: Currently this can only be done using the addExtraOption method.  There should be a type-safe set of methods for it, as with all other options.
The connection implementation should allow connection multiplexing, and higher levels of the driver should determine whether to take advantage of it based on the use case.

summary: DB.addUser javadoc comment refers to the wrong commands
description: The javadoc comment on the deprecated DB.addUser() methods refer to the "addUser" command rather than "createUser'

summary: Use an non-existing field to create index, it should not successfull.
description: When I create index, use a non-existing filed to create, it should throw exception, but the index has been created.

summary: No createIndex signature exists to match keyspec, name, unique
MongoClientURI does not support wtimeoutms query parameter
description:
https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-java-driver/blob/master/src/main/com/mongodb/MongoClientURI.java#L354

The java client respects wtimeout, not wtimeoutMS as the documentation says.

*Reporter*: Sverre Sundsdal
*E-mail*: [mailto:sverre@comoyo.com]

summary: JsonWriter produces invalid output for Longs under JsonMode.STRICT

description: Part of a larger effort to remove this property from all normal return values.
Part of a larger effort to remove this property from all normal return values.

summary: Allow count to work with query hints
description: MongoDB 2.6 added support for hinting for the count command.  Currently the Java driver does not pass the hint specified on DBCursor to that command.  It should start doing so.

summary: Java Driver can not update embedded array document
description:
this command:
db.collection.update( { <query selector> }, { <update operator>: { "array.$.field" : value } } )
use array.$.field" as BasicDBObject key, the exception say:

Exception in thread "main" java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Document field names can't have a . in them. (Bad Key: 'dimensionArray.$.v')
	at com.mongodb.DBCollection.validateKey(DBCollection.java:1843)
	at com.mongodb.DBCollection._checkKeys(DBCollection.java:1803)
	at com.mongodb.DBCollection._checkObject(DBCollection.java:1790)
	at com.mongodb.DBCollectionImpl.update(DBCollectionImpl.java:256)
	at com.mongodb.DBCollection.update(DBCollection.java:191)
	at com.mongodb.DBCollection.update(DBCollection.java:224)
DB.authenticate method is deprecated in 2.12, but still mentioned in the getting started guide.

summary: The flag "continueOnError" doesn't work when bulk insert
description:
I used flag "continueOnError" to bulk insert, but can't successful.  

The java code in the attachment.
Currently it uses java.util.Random()

summary: Make isMulti default to true in UpdateRequest
description: Make isMulti default to true in UpdateRequest

summary: Remove ReplaceOperation and ReplaceProtocol from core
description: UpdateOperation and UpdateProtocol should handle both update and replace.  This will allow a high level API to batch up mixtures of updates and replaces.

summary: findOne overload that takes only the value of the _id field does not apply read preference to mongos properly
description: DBCollection.findOne has an overload that takes just the value of the _id field and turns it into a regular query.  But the code path that it takes does not apply the read preference properly when connected to a mongos.

summary: ThreadLocal<Random> anonymous subclass prevents classloader from being garbage collected
description:
Quoting from Stefan's comment:

The problem is how BaseCluster.random is initialized to a ThreadLocal subclass instance. That class references the webapp's classloader. The ThreadLocal subclass in turn is referenced by each Thread instance (that's how ThreadLocals are implemented, they have a "helper-Map" in each Thread instance, so the leak is actually not a tiny Random instance but the whole webapp's classloader with a bunch of class definitions and statically referenced parts of the webapp

There's a somewhat mysterious StringRangeSet class, which appears to be used only for creating the numeric field names for BSON arrays. The proposal is to deprecate this in 2.13, as it will become non-public in 3.0.
This is no longer configurable.  The driver uses as many threads as there are servers to monitor.
This is no longer configurable.  The driver uses as many threads as there are servers to monitor.

summary: Authentication mechanism properties should be case insensitive
description: For instance, SERVICE_NAME and service_name refer to the same property
It will be deprecated in 2.13
